reviews.review_description: Love the feeling of being free to float above the earth and the mini games are also great. moving around the ISS is a wonderful experience. Thanks to the devs for this. Any chance of updating/dlc that lets us run a shuttle mission? Lift off to mission/return would just be breathtaking and your game engine is perfect for this.

developer_response.body:
Hello,
Thanks very much for the really positive feedback. It means a lot :-)
I've thought about adding on more elements, but there are already some apps out there that cover shuttle landing etc. That said, it would be nice to add .... :-)

reviews.review_description: I’m a newbie at this VR stuff, but this app is “otherworldly!”(pun intended). It’s about as close to actually floating in space as a non-astronaut will get! One question: using the settings I’ve chosen, the ISS, Earth and moon are fairly clear, but the stars are pretty blurry. Is there a fix for this?

developer_response.body: Hello. Thanks very much for the very kind review. I'll look to see if the resolution of the stars can be boosted. There's a limit to processing power, so I've prioritized the earth textures, which were quadrupled in the last release. But I'll take a look at the stars also :-)

reviews.review_description:
I love this game But It would be cool if you could add more planets.
also it would be cool if you could add a free fall from the ship to earth. mode.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the positive review. The only reason I haven't added more planets is that there are other apps that do tours of the universe. But I'll take a look and see if there is something I can add to these. Journey to earth is an interesting challenge :-)

reviews.review_description: I was just in a space  ship the hold time

developer_response.body:
I guess you didn't open the door to exit into space?

In the shuttle level, open the control panel using the controller buttons. There is a button there to open the shuttle doors. It's an icon of a shuttle with the doors open. 

In the ISS level, press the "open" icon on the wall of the airlock you start off in to open the door to space. 

Hopefully you'll enjoy the spacewalk app more when you get out to space. 😃

reviews.review_description: I’m overwhelmed with emotion, this is is just beautiful. Well done to the team

I’m a rookie  this was overwhelming for me lol I was scared to leave the shuttle! 
I’m a chicken rookie so if you’re new at all this VR stuff you’ll love this experience!

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the 5 star review! I really appreciate it. :-)

reviews.review_description: My current fave game to simply just zone out in and for such a cheap price its a proper bargain..great graphics and pretty good tunes...controls are nice n easy to master...still not delved to deep into the 3 different game modes yet but so far real good fun with the puzzles n saving earth ..cant say too much ..not attempted getting inside the space station yet if possible..this game has impressed me so much  ive literally just bought another of their creations gear out...and at full price too and why not!! Its to cheap as is ..nice
This app doesn't work. Says you're going to fix it, well...fix it

developer_response.body:
I've just downloaded and re-installed the app. It all seemed to work fine on my devices. Maybe try and download it again, in case you had a corrupted download or something.

I can see from general logs that there are some crashes happening where an older version of Space Walk is being used with the latest version of Oculus software, although I couldn't reproduce crashes on my devices. Ensure you have the latest version of Spacewalk to avoid this (V8xx).

If you contact me directly, I might be able to help if the issue is specific to your device. Or you might be able to provide information that exposes a wider issue that I am not seeing on my devices. Hitting the one-star button just trashes my reputation without giving me any information to help with.

You said "Says you're going to fix it" - I haven't written anything like that so perhaps you're commenting on the wrong app?

reviews.review_description:
The designers have done a magnificent  job of creating a realistic environment. You absolutely feel like your floating  in space   

There are several EVA missions or just float around and learn to use your controls. 

As long as you don’t get motion sickness, take off the stabilizers. With the stabilizers on it is easy to navigate.  Basically you turn on a jet you move stop jetting you stop. 

Turn off the stabilizers and you continue to float along. To stop you will need to reverse the thrust. It is way to easy to over correct and completely miss your target.   This makes it a really challenging game. 

I would like to be able to begat down my thrust control.  The trick is small corrections

This was one of the most amazing  things I have experienced. I was a child of the 60s saw the moon landing in 1969. I couldn't stop crying this was so profound for me a truly exceptional experience for me . Absolutely fabulous x

developer_response.body: Thank you soooo much! I'm really glad you are enjoying it.

reviews.review_title: Nice improvements from the Go version
reviews.review_description: I really like that you can position yourelf over any country and set the time of day and orbit right over. Very fun to look at areas of the country that you have lived. The night time view really makes this possible because you can see large towns lit up. I could even see Greeley, Colorado. The only thing I'd like to see added is a button that would align you with the earth's horizon. Moving around in space can create motion sickness. I would also like an option for slower locomotion so it's very smooth.

For example, I know the suit while space walking is correct, I wish my feet didn’t bounce into everything, I also wish that there was a way to boost from the way your hand is pointing 
Edit: So what I don’t understand is that if you are gripping why would you also be boosting? Maybe I just need to play seated

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for installing Space Walk and for the helpful feedback. There are two methods of boost at the moment. One is to use the joysticks but I prefer the method of pressing the trigger buttons which will boost you towards or away from where you are looking. I think the trouble with linking boost direction to hand position, is that you might be gripping something and that could mean you couldn't grip where you want and steer where you want at the same time. (Edit: This happens when you want to push something around, like the satellites for example).  I'll look into the feet issue - maybe tipping the jetpack forward will help.
